Build Instructions Building source Install ROS create workspace: Installing Configuring ROS Environment Move entire ntrip_client folder your_workspace/src directory. Install rosdeps package: rosdep install --from-paths ~/your_workspace/src --ignore-src Build workspace: ~/your_workspace catkin_make source ~/your_workspace/devel/setup.bash source command run terminal prior launching ROS node. Launch node publish data command launch node. mind instance run separate terminal. roslaunch ntrip_client ntrip_client.launch Optional launch parameters: host: Hostname address NTRIP server connect port: Port connect server. Default: mountpoint: Mountpoint connect NTRIP server. ntrip_version: Ntrip-Version header initial HTTP request caster. authenticate: authenticate server, send unauthenticated request. set true, username, password supplied. username: Username authenticating NTRIP server. authenticate true password: Password authenticating NTRIP server. authenticate true ssl: connect SSL. cert, key, ca_cert options effect true cert: NTRIP caster configured cert based authentication, option client certificate key: NTRIP caster configured cert based authentication, option private key ca_cert: NTRIP caster signed certs, chain, option file rtcm_message_packege: type ROS RTCM message published node. description: Object_recognition_msgs contains the ROS message and the actionlib definition used in object_recognition_core

The dynamicEDT3D library implements an inrementally updatable Euclidean distance transform (EDT) in 3D. It comes with a wrapper to use the OctoMap 3D representation and hooks into the change detection of the OctoMap library to propagate changes to the EDT.

The OctoMap library implements a 3D occupancy grid mapping approach, providing data structures and mapping algorithms in C++. The map implementation is based on an octree. See
  http://octomap.github.io for details.

Wikipedia details. twist.linear.x Local frame velocity vehicle twist.linear.y Local frame lateral velocity vehicle twist.angular.z Yaw rate vehicle rad/s imu/data (sensor_msgs/Imu) Orientation, angular rates, linear accelerations reported OxTS unit gps/pos_type (std_msgs/String) Position solution type reported OxTS unit (point position, differential pseudorange, RTK float, RTK integer, gps/nav_status (std_msgs/String) Navigation status reported OxTS unit. fix, vel, odom, imu/data topics published topic string “READY”. gps/time_ref (sensor_msgs/TimeReference) UTC equivalent GPS timestamp reported OxTS unit. Note work hardware abstraction robot. robot specific hard generalize. similar read() write() functions ROS-control, implement robot-specific protocols. package interfaces implement feature. Controller .yaml config controller joint trajectories robot forward_joint_trajectories: type: "pass_through_controllers/JointTrajectoryController" joints: joint1 joint2 joint3 joint4 joint5 joint6 controller joint trajectories robot forward_cartesian_trajectories: type: "pass_through_controllers/CartesianTrajectoryController" joints: joint1 joint2 joint3 joint4 joint5 joint6 Examples integration tests package give impression controller. ur_robot_driver controllers. Adapting RobotHW pass_through_controllers expect JointTrajectoryInterface CartesianTrajectoryInterface, depending trajectory Registering RobotHW #include <pass_through_controllers/trajectory_interface.h> class YourRobot public hardware_interface::RobotHW Callbacks pass_through_controllers' events void startJointInterpolation(const hardware_interface::JointTrajectory& trajectory); void startCartesianInterpolation(const hardware_interface::CartesianTrajectory& trajectory); void cancelJointInterpolation(); void cancelCartesianInterpolation(); interfaces trajectory forwarding hardware_interface::JointTrajectoryInterface jnt_traj_interface_; hardware_interface::CartesianTrajectoryInterface cart_traj_interface_; implementation: YourRobot::YourRobot() Register callbacks trajectory passthrough jnt_traj_interface_.registerGoalCallback( std::bind(&HardwareInterface::startJointInterpolation, std::placeholders::1)); jnt_traj_interface.registerCancelCallback(std::bind(&HardwareInterface::cancelInterpolation, cart_traj_interface_.registerGoalCallback( std::bind(&HardwareInterface::startCartesianInterpolation, std::placeholders::1)); cart_traj_interface.registerCancelCallback(std::bind(&HardwareInterface::cancelInterpolation, void YourRobot::startJointInterpolation(const hardware_interface::JointTrajectory& trajectory) Robot-specific implementation void YourRobot::startCartesianInterpolation(const hardware_interface::CartesianTrajectory& trajectory) File truncated lines full file

readme: DRACO POINT CLOUD TRANSPORT Plugin ROS package point_cloud_transport, Google Draco compression library low-bandwidth lossy transportation PointCloud2 messages. Publisher plugin dynamic reconfiguration parameters, change compression runtime. Draco encode invalid values, pointclouds invalid values turned unstructured dense clouds NaNs, height Encode Decode Speed adjusting encode_speed dedode_speed parameters, adjust speed plugin encodes point cloud. higher number, higher speed encoding/decoding, worse compression. detailed information, documentation Draco. Encode Method Auto method decides encoding method based parameter default method. KD-tree method forces encoder KD-tree encoding. attribute point cloud type float32, kd-tree encoding requires attribute quantized. Sequential method forces encoder sequential encoding. Quantization sequential encoding. Sequential encoding worse compression KD-tree, faster arrangement points point cloud intact. sequential encoding encode point clouds Kinect. Deduplicate Deduplicate option tells encoder delete duplicate points point cloud, allowing transport smaller point clouds. Force Quantization Force_quantization option forces quantization KD-tree encoding method. Quantization Attribute Types Quantization_POSITION, Quantization_NORMAL, Quantization_COLOR tells encoder bits quantization attribute type. Attribute type point cloud attribute recognized based list names: POSITION POSITION POSITION “pos” POSITION “position” POSITION “rgb” COLOR “rgba” COLOR COLOR COLOR COLOR COLOR NORMAL NORMAL NORMAL encoded GENERIC custom quantization, edit list recognized names expert_quantization expert_attribute_type options. Expert Quantization Expert_quantization option encoder custom quantization values point cloud attributes. Multiple POSITION attribute encoded varying quantization levels. set quantization PointField entry point cloud advertised base topic base_topic, set parameter: /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/quantization_bits/x. rosparam set /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/quantization_bits/x expert_quantization, user quantization bits PointField entries point cloud. Accepted types POSITION NORMAL COLOR TEX_COORD GENERIC encoding rgb/rgba COLOR, user common rgba tweak ROS (encoding rgba instances Byte instance float32). inform encoder, PointField entry “rgb” handled tweak, set parameter: rosparam set /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/rgba_tweak/rgb true Subscriber Set Skip Dequantization Attribute Types SkipDequantizationPOSITION, SkipDequantizationNORMAL, SkipDequantizationCOLOR options decoder skip dequantization attribute types.

Users will send a goal to an Action in the message format of PR2Gripper*Command (where * replaces the name of the particular Action from pr2_gripper_sensor_action). Feedback and Result information for the action is then returned in the format of PR2Gripper*Data.

description: quaternion operations
readme: pyquaternion fully featured, pythonic library quaternion representation, manipulation, animation geometry. visit pyquaternion homepage full information latest documentation. Designed Python Smooth animation interpolation random orientations pyquaternion module. Quickstart Install PyPI pip install pyquaternion Run basic overview. copy [demo.py](./demo/demo.py). ```python import pyquaternion Create quaternion representing rotation degrees positive axis. my_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, degrees=90) my_vector my_rotated_vector my_quaternion.rotate(my_vector) print('\nBasic Rotation') print('--------------') print('My Vector: {}'.format(my_vector)) print('Performing rotation {angle} deg {axis}'.format(angle=my_quaternion.degrees, axis=my_quaternion.axis)) print('My Rotated Vector: {}'.format(my_rotated_vector)) Create quaternion representing rotation null_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, angle=0) print('\nInterpolated Rotation') print('---------------------') create sequence intermediate quaternion rotation objects pyquaternion.Quaternion.intermediates(null_quaternion, my_quaternion, include_endpoints=True): my_interpolated_point q.rotate(my_vector) print('My Interpolated Point: {point}\t(after rotation {angle} deg {axis})'.format( point=my_interpolated_point, angle=round(q.degrees, axis=q.axis print('Done!') output: Basic Rotation Vector: Performing rotation deg Rotated Vector: Interpolated Rotation Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg

description:
This stack provides Python bindings for Qt.
    There are two providers: pyside and pyqt.  PySide is released under
    the LGPL.  PyQt is released under the GPL.

    Both the bindings and tools to build bindings are included from each
    available provider.  For PySide, it is called "Shiboken".  For PyQt,
    this is called "SIP".

    Also provided is adapter code to make the user's Python code
    independent of which binding provider was actually used which makes
    it very easy to switch between these.

description: razor_imu_9dof is a package that provides a ROS driver for the Sparkfun OpenLog Artemis, 9DoF Razor IMU M0, 9DOF Razor IMU and 9DOF Sensor Stick. It also provides Arduino firmware that runs on the board, and which must be installed on it for the system to work. A node which displays the attitude (roll, pitch and yaw) of the board (or any IMU) is provided for testing.
authors: Tang Tiong Yew, Kristof Robot, Paul Bouchier, Peter Bartz

description:
The rc_dynamics_api provides an API for easy handling of the dynamic-state data
      streams provided by Roboception's stereo camera with self-localization.
      See http://rc-visard.com

      Dynamic-state estimates of the rc_visard relate to its self-localization and
      ego-motion estimation. These states refer to rc_visard's current pose,
      velocity, or acceleration and are published on demand via several data streams.
      For a complete list and descriptions of these dynamics states and the
      respective data streams please refer to rc_visard's user manual.
authors: Heiko Hirschmueller, Christian Emmerich, Felix Endres

description:
GenICam/GigE Vision Convenience Layer.

      This package combines the Roboception convenience layer for images with the
      GenICam reference implementation and a GigE Vision transport layer. It is a
      self contained package that permits configuration and image streaming of
      GenICam / GigE Vision 2.0 compatible cameras like the Roboception rc_visard.

      This package also provides some tools that can be called from the command line
      for discovering cameras, changing their configuration and streaming images.
      Although the tools are meant to be useful when working in a shell or in a
      script, their main purpose is to serve as example on how to use the API for
      reading and setting parameters, streaming and synchronizing images.

      See LICENSE.md for licensing terms of the different parts.

description: The rc_hand_eye_calibration_client package
readme: ROS Hand-eye calibration client Roboception’s rc_visard package developed anymore. client rc_reason_clients_ros repository. node ROS service calls topics calibrate rc_visard robot (aka hand-eye calibration). pre-existing calibration /tf_static. default behavior request existing calibration rc_visard startup, broadcast (latched) /tf_static broadcast advertised ROS services calibrate get_calibration called. calibration routine consists steps: Setting calibration parameters, grid size mounting, dynamic reconfigure. user-defined number robot calibration poses repeat Move robot pose (calibration grid visible rc_visard’s view). Send robot pose rc_visard (set_pose) Trigger calibration tranformation calculated (calibrate). calibration transform calculated tested, saved rc_visard (save_calibration). detailed instructions calibration routine consult rc_visard manual: https://doc.rc-visard.com. Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-hand-eye-calibration-client Source package relies git submodules cpr library initialized building source. git submodule update --init --recursive Configuration Parameters device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline setting rosparam https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). neccessary string launch file. user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors. host: device address hostname rc_visard calibrated. rc_visard_frame_id: frame rc_visard calibrating. Default: “camera” end_effector_frame_id: frame calibrated robot_mounted rc_visard. Default: “end_effector”. base_frame_id: frame calibrated statically (externally) mounted rc_visard (robot_mounted false). Default: “base_link” calibration_request_period: Decimal number, controls requesting calibration rc_visard. Default: positive: Interval seconds automated requests latest calibration. request startup (default). negative, calibration requested. case, results broadcast services calibrate get_calibration node called. calibration_publication_period: Decimal number, controls broadcasting calibration /tf_static. Default: positive: Interval seconds. negative calibration broadcast /tf_static changed manual periodic calibration request (default). Dynamic reconfigure parameters grid_width: width calibration pattern meters. grid_height: height calibration pattern meters. robot_mounted: camera mounted robot tcp_rotation_axis:, TCP rotation axis DOF robot calibration general robot) tcp_offset: Offset TCP DOF robot calibration Services services offered follow calibration routine: reset_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Deletes previously poses images. saved calibration result reloaded. service (re-)start hand-eye calibration scratch. set_pose [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibrationPose]: robot pose calibration pose hand-eye calibration routine. calibrate [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Calculates returns hand-eye calibration transformation robot poses configured set_pose service. Broadcasts result /tf_static. get_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Returns existing hand-eye calibration transformation. Broadcasts result /tf_static. save_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Persistently saves result hand-eye calibration rc_visard overwrites existing stored result retrieved time get_calibration service. remove_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Removes stored hand-eye calibration rc_visard. call get_calibration service reports hand-eye calibration Periodic broadcasting stopped. set_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibration]: Sets hand-eye calibration transformation. calibration transformation expected format returned calibrate get_calibration calls. save_calibration called calibration transformation persistent. Launching command line parameters: rosrun rc_hand_eye_calibration_client rc_hand_eye_calibration_client_node _device:=:<serial_number>

description:
This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

Launch files to open an RGBD device and load all nodelets to 
     convert raw depth/RGB/IR streams to depth images, disparity images, 
     and (registered) point clouds.
Controllers for Ridgeback
URDF robot description for Ridgeback
Messages exclusive to Ridgeback, especially for representing low-level motor commands and sensors.

A fork of hector_gazebo_plugins to provide the ros_force_based_move plugin.

description: The robot_navigation package

The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.

readme: Hardware Interfaces Hardware interfaces ROS control conjunction ROS controllers send (\ref hardware_interface::RobotHW::write) commands hardware receive (\ref hardware_interface::RobotHW::read) states robot’s resources (joints, sensors, actuators). list hardware interfaces HardwareResourceManager) writing JointCommandInterface: hardware interface support commanding reading state array joints. Note commands semantic meaning long represented single double, necessarily effort commands. meaning command, derived classes: EffortJointInterface: commanding reading effort-based joints. VelocityJointInterface: commanding reading velocity-based joints. PositionJointInterface: commanding reading position-based joints. JointStateInterfaces: hardware interface support reading state array named joints, position, velocity, effort (force torque). ActuatorStateInterfaces: hardware interface support reading state array named actuators, position, velocity, effort (force torque). ActuatorCommandInterfaces EffortActuatorInterface VelocityActuatorInterface PositionActuatorInterface PosVelJointInterface PosVelAccJointInterface Force-torque sensor Interface IMU sensor Interface Note \ref hardware_interface::JointCommandInterface reading joint state commanding [effort|velocity|position]-based joints answer).

Enforcing limits overwrite commands set controllers, operate separate raw data buffer. main elements involved setting joint limits interface: Joint limits representation JointLimits Position, velocity, acceleration, jerk effort. SoftJointLimits Soft position limits, Loading URDF convenience methods loading joint limits information (position, velocity effort), soft joint limits information URDF. Loading ROS params convenience methods loading joint limits ROS parameter server (position, velocity, acceleration, jerk effort).Parameter specification MoveIt, addition parse jerk effort limits. Examples refer joint_limits_interface wiki page.

readme: Transmission Interface Overview transmission_interface data structures representing mechanical transmissions, methods propagating position, velocity effort variables actuator joint spaces. spirit hardware_interface package, package wraps existing raw data current actuator position, reference joint command, consistent interface. imposing specific layout raw data, easier support arbitrary hardware drivers software control. Structure main elements involved setting transmission_interface: Transmission class defines abstract interface mapping position, velocity effort variables actuator joint space. Derived classes implement specific transmission types, simple reducers, differentials four-bar linkages. Note single transmission couple variables multiple actuators joints differential couples actuators joints). TransmissionHandle class associates Transmission instance set raw data variables operates ActuatorData JointData structures). Derived classes implement specific maps, JointToActuatorEffortHandle ActuatorToJointStateHandle. **TransmissionInterface** class manages set transmission handles type. Note handles type, underlying transmissions heterogeneous single **ActuatorToJointPositionInterface** set transform position variables actuator joint space arm four-bar-linkage shoulder, differential wrist, simple reducers Examples refer transmission_interface wiki page.

description:
Defines a hardware interface to send Cartesian commands to a robot hardware and read
    Cartesian states.
readme: Cartesian Interface package hardware interfaces implement Cartesian ROS control robot manipulators. Rationale OEMs provide native Cartesian interfaces robot drivers. offering hardware interfaces, implementers ROS controllers direct access Cartesian buffers reading writing. advantages classic joint-based interfaces depends personal cases. main difference Cartesian case robot takes care Inverse Kinematics. Usage package PoseCommandInterface TwistCommandInterface read/write access CartesianStateInterface pure read access. add hardware abstraction #include <cartesian_interface/cartesian_command_interface.h> #include <cartesian_interface/cartesian_state_handle.h> class YourRobot public hardware_interface::RobotHW interfaces Cartesian ROS-controllers ros_controllers_cartesian::CartesianStateInterface cart_interface_; ros_controllers_cartesian::TwistCommandInterface twist_interface_; ros_controllers_cartesian::PoseCommandInterface pose_interface_; Buffers read/write access geometry_msgs::Pose cart_pose_; geometry_msgs::Twist cart_twist_; geometry_msgs::Accel cart_accel_; geometry_msgs::Accel cart_jerk_; geometry_msgs::Twist twist_command_; geometry_msgs::Pose pose_command_; Registering YourRobot::YourRobot() ros_controllers_cartesian::CartesianStateHandle cart_state_handle("base", "tip", &cart_pose_, &cart_twist_, &cart_accel_, &cart_jerk_); cart_interface_.registerHandle(cart_state_handle); twist_interface_.registerHandle( ros_controllers_cartesian::TwistCommandHandle(cart_interface_.getHandle("tip"), &twist_command_)); twist_interface_.getHandle("tip"); pose_interface_.registerHandle( ros_controllers_cartesian::PoseCommandHandle(cart_interface_.getHandle("tip"), &pose_command_)); pose_interface_.getHandle("tip"); Register interfaces registerInterface(&twist_interface_); registerInterface(&pose_interface_); Note strings base tip instantiation Cartesian state handle. represent frames robot kinematics names vary robot robot. convention assumes base reference frame tip tip end-point robot control represents unique identifier handle. resource names joint-based ROS control. Cartesian ROS controllers implementing ROS controller, add handle read/write access, case Cartesian pose control: #include <cartesian_interface/cartesian_command_interface.h> class YourController public controller_interface::Controller<ros_controllers_cartesian::PoseCommandInterface> private: ros_controllers_cartesian::PoseCommandHandle handle_; instantiation handle, end-point control: handle_ hw->getHandle("tip"); handle implement control loop update(...): current state robot hardware geometry_msgs::Pose pose handle_.getPose(); geometry_msgs::Twist twist handle_.getTwist(); File truncated lines full file

description: A Cartesian trajectory controller with multiple hardware interface support
readme: Cartesian Trajectory Controller simple Cartesian trajectory controller Cartesian trajectory definition. Rationale controller started executing Cartesian trajectories robot. implements simple action server FollowCartesianTrajectory. ways execution, depending interface robot. table highlights applications. Hardware interface Primary application PoseCommandInterface spline interpolation ROS OEMs driver care inverse kinematics variant requires Cartesian interfaces. PositionJointInterface spline interpolation ROS implement solver. established Weighted Levenberg-Marquardt solver form KDL. variant compatible current ROS-control change RobotHW abstraction. Controller .yaml config Universal Robots controller Cartesian pose interface control. Inverse Kinematics handled robot pose_cartesian_traj_controller: type: "pose_controllers/CartesianTrajectoryController" type names driver convention. neccessarily exist robot_description correct control handle hardware interface. base: "base" tip: "tool0_controller" joints: shoulder_pan_joint shoulder_lift_joint elbow_joint wrist_1_joint wrist_2_joint wrist_3_joint controller joint position interface control. Inverse Kinematics handled controller. jnt_cartesian_traj_controller: type: "position_controllers/CartesianTrajectoryController" type requires valid URDF links set-up internal kinematics chain. correspond drivers' frames. case, tool0 tool0_controller identical, exist URDF description. base: "base" tip: "tool0" joints: shoulder_pan_joint shoulder_lift_joint elbow_joint wrist_1_joint wrist_2_joint wrist_3_joint Interpolation waypoints Cartesian trajectory controller builds joint_trajectory_controller’s quintic spline library. library internally wrapped work Cartesian space dimensions inherits essential interpolation mechanisms: poses linear interpolation poses twists cubic spline poses, twists accelerations quintic spline succeeding waypoints specifications waypoint pose-only, waypoint pose-twist), lowest common specification (pose-only Note: means default ROS message initializer, waypoints’ twist acceleration fields zero-initialized. zeros interpreted intended boundary conditions velocity accelerations level. consequence, pose-only Cartesian trajectories lead smooth stops waypoint default quintic interpolation velocity/acceleration boundaries). linear behavior pose-only Cartesian trajectory, don’t stops, mark twist field values uninitialized setting NaN equivalent. examples client code: #include <cartesian_control_msgs/CartesianTrajectoryPoint.h> cartesian_control_msgs::CartesianTrajectoryPoint Fill p.pose p.twist.linear.x std::nan("0"); declare p.twist uninitialized. description: Metapackage for Cartesian ROS controllers

A ros_control controller accepting Cartesian twist messages in order to move a robot manipulator.
    It uses a Cartesian interface to the robot, so that the robot hardware takes care about
    doing the inverse kinematics. This could be used e.g. for visual servoing applications.
readme: Twist Controller Cartesian ROS-controller commanding target twists robot. controller TwistCommandInterface set user twist message reference robot control. Controller .yaml configuration twist_controller: type: ros_controllers_cartesianlers/TwistController controller identifier command handle hardware interface. frame_id: "endpoint_to_control" joints: joint1 joint2 joint3 joint4 joint5 joint6 Acknowledgement Developed collaboration Supported ROSIN ROS-Industrial Quality-Assured Robot Software Components. information: rosin-project.eu project received funding European Union’s Horizon research innovation programme grant agreement

description: Contains aligned allocation functions, as well as an STL-compatible AlignedAllocator class.

The lockfree package contains lock-free data structures for use in multithreaded programming.  These
     kinds of data structures are generally not as easy to use as single-threaded equivalents, and are not
     always faster.  If you don't know you need to use one, try another structure with a lock around it
     first.
The ros_realtime package

rosatomic provides the C++11-style atomic operations by pulling symbols from the proposed Boost.Atomic
     package into the ros namespace.  Once C++11-style atomics (std::atomic) are available from compilers, rosatomic will
     conditionally use those instead.
rosrt provides classes for interfacing with ROS from within realtime systems, such as realtime-safe Publisher and Subscriber classes.

The rt_usb_9axisimu_driver package
readme: English rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driverは株式会社アールティが販売しているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュール用のROSパッケージです。 License: 3-Clause BSD License Source: https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git (branch: master) 現在、以下のROSのディストリビューションに対応しております。 Kinetic (kinetic-devel) Melodic (melodic-devel) Noetic (noetic-devel) Dashing (dashing-devel) Foxy (foxy-devel) industrial_ci source build amd64 binary arm64 binary main develop(master) ROS develop(ros2-devel) Xenial Kinetic(kinetic-devel) Bionic Melodic(melodic-devel) Focal Noetic(noetic-devel) Bionic Dashing(dashing-devel) Focal Foxy(foxy-devel) このパッケージは、USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールをROSから使用するためのドライバを提供するものです。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールは、センサとしてInvenSense社のMPU9250を使用しております。 このセンサの磁気センサの座標系はNED座標系(x-north, y-east, z-down)ですが、 モジュール内のマイコン(LPC1343)においてENU座標系(x-east, y-north, z-up)に変換され、 これはROSで使われる座標系のルールにも適合しています。詳しくは、REP-0103をご覧ください。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはオープンハード・オープンソースのため、モジュール内のマイコンのファームウェアの変更が可能です。 このROSパッケージはデフォルトのファームウェアにのみ対応しております。ファームウェアを変更された場合、正常な動作ができなくなる恐れがございますので、ご了承ください。 ver2.0でのご利用について 2018年10月現在、販売されているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはver2.0となります。 このバージョンのデフォルトのファームウェアには、ASCII出力とBinary出力の２つのデータ出力形式があります。 センサ出荷時点ではASCII出力に設定されています。出力形式の切り替え方法は、以下のリポジトリにあるマニュアルをご参照ください。 https://github.com/rt-net/RT-USB-9AXIS-00 ROS 2のパッケージについてはros2-develのREADMEをご覧ください。 ROS Kineticの場合 sudo apt install ros-kinetic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver ROS Melodicの場合 sudo apt install ros-melodic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver catkinワークスペースを~/catkin_wsとすると、以下のような手順になります。 ~/catkin_ws/src git clone $ROS_DISTRO-devel https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git ~/catkin_ws catkin build 2のようにインストールを行った場合、ドライバを起動しようとする端末(Terminal)で、 source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash roscore rosrun rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver roslaunch rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.launch [ERROR] Error opening sensor device, re-check devices. This package provides an interface for operating the schunk dexterous hand (SDH), including the tactile sensors.

This package provides simulated tactile sensors for the Schunk Dextrous
     Hand (SDH) which is mounted on the Care-O-bot arm. The node subscribes to
     the Gazebo bumper topics of the SDH. It transforms the Gazebo feedback to
     the "tactile_data" topic to provide the same tactile sensor interface as
     the schunk_sdh package.

     The following parameters can be set:
     * cells_x: The number of patches on the tactile sensor in the direction
                perpendicular to the finger. Defaults to 6.
     * cells_y: The number of patches on the tactile sensor along the direction
                of the finger. Defaults to 14.
     * output_range: The maximum output value of one patch. Defaults to 3500.
     * sensitivity: The change of output in one patch per Newton. Defaults to
                    350. The sensitivity can be approximated by the following
                    formula: S = output_range / (measurement_range * cell_area)
                    - The measurement range of the tactile pads is 250 kPa (from
                      the data sheet).
                    - The output range can be determined by experiment from the
                      real SDH. It is about 3500.
                    - The cell area is the size of one patch. Length and width
                      of the area are determined by dividing the length/width
                      of the collision surface by the number of cells in the
                      respective direction.
                      Important: In most cases this is NOT the cell area that is
                                 given in the data sheet!
     * filter_length: The length of the moving average filter which smoothes
                      the values from simulation. Defaults to 10.

     The node subscribes to the following topics to receive data from the
     simulation:
     * thumb_2/state
     * thumb_3/state
     * finger_12/state
     * finger_13/state
     * finger_22/state
     * finger_23/state

     The node publishes the processed data on the following topic:
     * tactile_data

     The simulated bumper must obtain the collision data in the link that the
     sensor is attached to. This is achieved by setting the "frameName" property
     in the gazebo_ros_bumper controller.

description: Package for using SkyWay from ROS
readme: SkyWay ROS ROSからSkyWayを利用するためのパッケージです。 端末間でP2P接続を行うための規格としてWebRTCが標準化されています。 SkyWayはWebRTCプラットフォームであり、SkyWayを利用することで、ブラウザ・iOS・Androidなどの環境で相互にWebRTC接続を簡単に行うことができます。 WebRTCは映像と音声を双方向通信するためのMediaStreamと、データを転送するためのDataChannelが定義されています。 Skyway ROSを利用することで、MediaStreamとDataChannelをROSパッケージから利用することができます。 SkyWay ROSでは2つのサービスが提供されており、これらを利用し操作を行うことで、外部プログラム(Peer)に対してMediaStreamとDataConnection(over DataChannel)の確立を行います。 SkyWayControl SkyWayEvents SkyWay ROS内のMediaSocketに対してRTP/RTCPを送信することで、確立されたMedia Connectionを経由して、Peerに対して映像と音声を送ることができます。 また、データ転送用のROS Pluginをユーザ側で定義することができ、そのPluginに対してROS Topic, Service, Actionなどを用いてデータを送受信することで、 Peerとデータのやりとりを行うことができます。 操作方法とAPI仕様 まずSkyWay ROS内に、SkyWayサーバとやり取りするために　Peer Objectを生成します。以下のドキュメントを参照してください。 PeerObjectの生成 PeerObjectの状態確認 Peer Objectが生成できたら、MediaConnection, DataConnectionの接続処理及び待ち受けが行えるようになります。以下のページを参照してください。 MediaConnectionの確立 MediaConnectionの待ち受け DataConnectionの確立 DataConnectionの待ち受け DataConnectionが確立できたら、Pluginを介して外部ROS Moduleとデータのやり取りを行えます。 Pluginの仕様についてはこちらのドキュメントを参照してください。 tipsも参照して下さい。 examplesディレクトリ及びskyway_for_ros_examplesリポジトリを参照して下さい。

description:
This package contains a ROS wrapper for OpenSlam's Gmapping. 
  The gmapping package provides laser-based SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping), 
  as a ROS node called slam_gmapping. Using slam_gmapping, you can create a 2-D occupancy
  grid map (like a building floorplan) from laser and pose data collected by a mobile robot.
slam_gmapping contains a wrapper around gmapping which provides SLAM capabilities.

The teb_local_planner package implements a plugin
    to the base_local_planner of the 2D navigation stack.
    The underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes
    the robot's trajectory with respect to trajectory execution time,
    separation from obstacles and compliance with kinodynamic constraints at runtime.

description:
The ROS packages in this repository were created to provide an improved
    alternative Inverse Kinematics solver to the popular inverse Jacobian
    methods in KDL.  TRAC-IK handles joint-limited chains better than KDL
    without increasing solve time.
This package contains the source code for testing and comparing trac_ik

description:
TRAC-IK is a faster, significantly more reliable drop-in replacement for
    KDL's pseudoinverse Jacobian solver.

    The TRAC-IK library has a very similar API to KDL's IK solver calls,
    except that the user passes a maximum time instead of a maximum number of
    search iterations.  Additionally, TRAC-IK allows for error tolerances to
    be set independently for each Cartesian dimension (x,y,z,roll,pitch.yaw).
readme: TRAC-IK kinematics solver built trac_ik_lib library tested ROS Indigo Catkin). headers shared objects package linked user programs. package part ROS Noetic binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-trac-ik package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik requires Ubuntu packages NLOpt Libraries installed ros-indigo-nlopt packages proper headers). running sudo apt-get install libnlopt-dev trusty standard Ubuntu distros. Alternatively, run ```rosdep update rosdep install trac_ik_lib KDL KDL::ChainFkSolverPos_recursive fk_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverVel_pinv vik_solver(chain); KDL::ChainJntToJacSolver jac_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverPos_NR_JL ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, fk_solver, vik_solver, int num_iterations, double error); int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: timeout seconds iterations set called loop timer. NOTE: error acceptable purposes TRAC-IK: #include <trac_ik/trac_ik.hpp> TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(string base_link, string tip_link, string URDF_param="/robot_description", double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); NOTE: arguments constructors optional. type Speed: returns quickly solution Distance: runs full timeout_in_secs, returns solution minimizes SSE seed Manip1: runs full timeout, returns solution maximizes sqrt(det(J*J^T)) product singular values Jacobian) Manip2: runs full timeout, returns solution minimizes ratio min max singular values Jacobian. int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints, KDL::Twist tolerances); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: tolerances effector pose optional, default ABS() values set tolerances -tol..0..+tol Cartesian dimensions effector pose.

This repository includes ros packages to plan routes for multiple robots on a search graph. It creates a search graph out of a pixel map and tries to find a path for multiple robots using an extended approach for prioritized planning. The inputs are the tuw_multi_robot_msgs/RobotInfo messages which include the robots pose, the map and the desired goal poses. The output are multiple synchronized routes given to the individual robots.
maintainers: Benjamin Binder, Markus Bader, Florian Beck

description:
The tuw_multi_robot_goal_generator package was designed to generate, store, and read goal lists for the tuw_multi_robot_router.
    The goal_saver is able to store publish goal msgs of type tuw_multi_robot_msgs::RobotGoalsArray in a human readable form. 
    The goal_server is able to read and publish this msgs and to read patches of saved goals for testing.
    The goals_random node subscribes to the occupancy grid and is able to auto generate valid goals for testing.

This contains CvBridge, which converts between ROS
    Image messages and OpenCV images.

`image_geometry` contains C++ and Python libraries for interpreting images
    geometrically. It interfaces the calibration parameters in sensor_msgs/CameraInfo
    messages with OpenCV functions such as image rectification, much as cv_bridge
    interfaces ROS sensor_msgs/Image with OpenCV data types.
Tests the enumerants of the ROS Image message, and functionally tests the Python and C++ implementations of CvBridge.
Packages for interfacing ROS with OpenCV, a library of programming functions for real time computer vision.
Virtual package providing ViSP related packages.

Online automated pattern-based object tracker relying on visual servoing.

    visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers provided by ViSP visual
    servoing library into a ROS package. The tracked object should have a
    QRcode, Flash code, or April tag pattern. Based on the pattern, the object is
    automaticaly detected. The detection allows then to initialise the
    model-based trackers. When lost of tracking achieves a new detection
    is performed that will be used to re-initialize the tracker.

    This computer vision algorithm computes the pose (i.e. position and
    orientation) of an object in an image. It is fast enough to allow
    object online tracking using a camera.
readme: visp_auto_tracker visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers ViSP visual servoing library ROS package. tracked object QRcode, Flash code April tag pattern. Based pattern, object automaticaly detected. detection initialise model-based trackers. lost tracking achieves detection performed re-initialize tracker. computer vision algorithm computes pose position orientation) object image. fast object online tracking camera. package composed node called ‘visp_auto_tracker’. node detect QRcode, Flash April tag code object. detection performed, node tracks object. lost tracking occurs node detect object restart tracking. viewer comming visp_tracker package monitor tracking result. Project webpage ros.org: tutorial API reference Project webpage: source code download, bug report Setup package submodules. compiled ROS package catkin_make. Prerequisities visp_auto_tracker depends visp_bridge visp_tracker packages https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp (indigo-devel branches). visp_auto_tracker depends libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev system dependencies. install run: sudo apt-get install libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev build visp_tracker Supposed catkin work space run: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone indigo-devel https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp.git catkin_make --pkg visp_auto_tracker Documentation documentation project ROS homepage. information, refer ROS tutorial.

description:
This package contains nodes which make up the Passive-Scene-Recognition interface to Implicit Shape Model (ISM) trees. The Active-Scene-Recognition interface to ISM trees is located in asr_recognizer_prediction_ism, instead. A short outline of the functionalities provided by the nodes are:
      1. Recording of scenes
      2. Training of an ISM tree (Implicit shape model tree)
      3. Recognition of scenes
      4. Visualization of ISM (tree) data 
	  etc.

description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in

description:
This package contains a system to recognize scenes called the Probabilistic Scene Model (PSM).
    It uses objects and relative poses (relations) between the objects. The realations can be dynamic and each object can be a reference object.
    The system consists of a training subsystem that trains new scenes and a scene inference subsystem that calculates scene probabilities of previously trained scenes.
authors: Braun Kai, Gehrung Joachim, Heizmann Heinrich, Meißner Pascal
readme: General Acquisition: Call "start_cameras.launch" "start_recognition_system.launch" start add required nodes visualization, cameras, coordinate transformations object detectors. record data processing online learning scene models), call script "start_recording_localizer_input_and_results.sh SEQUENCE_ID" "start_recording_objects_only.sh SEQUENCE_ID". resulting file called "OBJ_SEQUENCE_ID.bag". Scene Model Learning: scene: Call "roslaunch asr_psm start_scene_graph_generation.launch scene_id:=SCENE_NAME" launch directory. scene graph generator started launch file collects published object messages processes means online data rosbag file Press key 'p' publish results. account, object type scene supported. holds objects type multiple trajectory recordings scene. script "start_recording_scene_graph.sh SEQUENCE_ID" scripts directory capture output. resulting file called "SCENEGRAPH_SEQUENCE_ID.bag". learn model bagfile(s) launch file "learner.launch" launch "roslaunch asr_psm learner.launch". Inference: Start image aquisition "roslaunch asr_psm start_cameras.launch". Start object detectors "roslaunch asr_psm start_recognition_system.launch". Edit "inference.launch" file fit launch command "roslaunch asr_psm inference.launch". results visualized RVIZ, demand gnuplot graph showing scene probabilities summoned.

description:
This package includes the algorithmic link between object pose prediction and scene recognition, both based on ISM trees. While it only refers to other packages with respect to the aforementioned
    functionalities, it includes the algorithms realizing Scene Model Sampling. E.g., it provides a (service-based) interface for the scene recognition, including an ASR-optimized visualization of ISM trees.
    The reason for linking scene recognition and object pose prediction within a package instead of message passing, is the size and number of scene recognition results occurring.
authors: Aumann Florian, Heller Florian, Hutmacher Robin, Meißner Pascal, Stöckle Patrick, Stroh Daniel
readme: asr_recognizer_prediction_ism documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_recognizer_prediction_ism

description: This package provides object pose prediction on the basis of the alternative scene model PSM (Probabilistic Scene Model): It generates hypotheses (type and pose) for missing objects in the context of 3D object search. Moreover, it integrates scene recognition and object pose prediction (both on the basis of PSM) into Active Scene Recognition. Its output are asr_next_best_view/AttributedPointCloud.msg messages which can be processed by asr_next_best_view.

description:
This package implements the logic which connects different functionalities such as (passive) scene recognition, object pose prediction and Next-Best-View estimation
    to Active Scene Recognition. In particular, it is the interface for executing different 3D-object-search procedures one of which Active Scene Recognition is.
    Active Scene Recognition is equivalent to the "object search", which consists of the "direct search" and "indirect search" modes. The search will be executed by
    using different packages of the asr framework.

description:
This package implements the logic of our Online World Model, while the Offline Knowledge Base is distributed across asr_object_database, mild_navigation and asr_visualization_server. It contains four types of information:
      1. All estimations of objects which were localized by object detection (object models)
      2. All views in which objects of given types have been searched due to advice of asr_next_best_view and asr_direct_search_manager
      3. All recognized complete patterns by the recognizer_prediction_ism (scene models)
      4. Some additional information about the Objects: IntermediateObjectWeight, RecognizerName

Visit AWS RoboMaker website learn building intelligent robotic applications Amazon Web Services. Install source code workspace. Add .rosinstall file contents workspace folder run rosws update: git: {local-name: src/aws-robomaker-simulation-ros-pkgs, uri: 'https://github.com/aws-robotics/aws-robomaker-simulation-ros-pkgs.git', version: master} Add dependency package.xml access service types (.srv): <package> <depend>aws_robomaker_simulation_ros_pkgs</depend> </package> Follow Python usage build bundle workspace usual: build ROS rosws update rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src colcon build colcon bundle Usage tag, untag, list tags simulation job ROS command-line ROS application running. AWS RoboMaker documentation. IAM role permissions Replace account# account number. "Version": "Statement": "Action": "robomaker:TagResource", "robomaker:UntagResource", "robomaker:ListTagsForResource" "Resource": "arn:aws:robomaker:*:account#:simulation-job/*" "Effect": AddTags Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.msg import Tag robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import AddTags def add_tags_to_my_sim(): add_tags([Tag(key="name", value="my_test")]) def add_tags(tags): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/add_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestAddTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/add_tags', AddTags) response requestAddTags(tags) response.success: AddTags failed print response.message RemoveTag Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import RemoveTags def remove_tags_from_my_sim(): remove_tags([Tag(key="name", value="my_test")]) def remove_tags(tags): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/remove_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestRemoveTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/remove_tags', RemoveTags) response requestRemoveTags(tags) response.success: RemoveTags failed print response.message File truncated lines full file

This package contains a recent version of the Bayesian Filtering
  Library (BFL), distributed by the Orocos Project.  For stability
  reasons, this package is currently locked to revision 31655 (April
  19, 2010), but this revision will be updated on a regular basis to
  the latest available BFL trunk.  This ROS package does not modify
  BFL in any way, it simply provides a convenient way to download and
  compile the library, because BFL is not available from an OS package
  manager.  This ROS package compiles BFL with the Boost library for
  matrix operations and random number generation.
authors: Klaas Gadeyne, Wim Meeussen, Tinne Delaet and many others. See web page for a full contributor list. ROS package maintained by Wim Meeussen.

GNU General Public License details. received copy GNU General Public License program; write Free Software Foundation, Temple Place Suite Boston, USA. library encoporates ideas software libraries: Scene (Andrew Davison). <http://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~ajd/Scene/> Bayes++ ACFR). <http://www.acfr.usyd.edu.au/technology/bayesianfilter/Bayes++.htm> CES programming library (Sebastian Thrun). <http://www-2.cs.cmu.edu/afs/cs.cmu.edu/user/thrun/public_html/papers/thrun.ces-tr.html> research Bayesian methods compliant motion problems <http://www.mech.kuleuven.be/pma/research/manip/default_en.phtml> important features Released GNU LGPL licence Wrapper matrix RNG libraries, favourite matrix library. wrappers exist matrix/RNG wrapper library LTIlib <http://ltilib.sourceforge.net/doc/homepage/index.shtml>: library algorithms data structures frequently image processing computer vision. NEWMAT <http://www.robertnz.net/nm_intro.htm> Matrix Library boost <http://www.boost.org/> RNG "Bayesian unifying Design". incorporate Bayesian filtering algorithm! filter schemes implemented. Standard EKF, IEKF Non-minimal State <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~tlefebvr/publicaties/BayesStat.ps.gz> Standard Particle filter (arbitrary proposal), BootstrapFilter (Proposal System Model PDF), Auxiliary Particle filter, Extended Kalman Particle Filter. details design ideas, poster library presented Valencia conference Bayesian Statistics, <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~kgadeyne/doctoraat.html> filtering libraries home page <http://www.orocos.org/bfl> Tinne Laet Contributed tutorial website. <http://people.mech.kuleuven.be/~tdelaet/bfl_doc/getting_started_guide/getting_started_guide.html> discusses construct filter bfl. Copy 99-px4fmu.rules /lib/udev/rules.d folder: ~/catkin_ws/src/clover/clover/config sudo 99-px4fmu.rules /lib/udev/rules.d Alternatively change fcu_url property mavros.launch file point flight controller device. Running start connection flight controller, roslaunch clover clover.launch simulation information article. Note package configured connect /dev/px4fmu default previous section). Install udev rules path FCU device mavros.launch.

description: Blockly programming support for Clover
readme: clover_blocks Blockly programming support Clover. user documentation main Clover documentation site. Internal package documentation Frontend frontend files located www subdirectory. frontend application roblib.js library communicating backend node ROS resources. clover_blocks node clover_blocks is blocks programming backend, implementing services topics needed running Blockly-generated Python script. Services ~run (clover_blocks/Run) run Blockly-generated program Python). ~stop (std_srvs/Trigger) terminate running program. ~store (clover_blocks/load) store user program <package_path>/programs default). ~load (clover_blocks/load) load stored programs. Parameters ~programs_dir (string) – directory user programs. Parameters read frontend: ~navigate_tolerance (float) distance tolerance meters, navigate-like blocks (default: ~yaw_tolerance (float) yaw angle tolerance degrees, set_yaw block (default: ~sleep_time (float) duration sleep loop cycles, navigate-like blocks (default: ~confirm_run (bool) enable confirmation run program (default: true). parameters set URL GET-parameters, http://<hostname>/clover_blocks/?navigate_tolerance=0.5&sleep_time=0.1 Topics Published ~running (std_msgs/Bool) program running. ~block (std_msgs/String) current executing block (maximum topic rate limited). ~error (std_msgs/String) – user program errors exceptions. ~prompt (clover_blocks/Prompt) user input request (includes random request string). topic published frontend side: ~prompt/<request_id> (std_msgs/String) – user input response.

Launching simulation Simulation launched simulator.launch file. .launch file assumes sitl_gazebo packages reachable current workspace. simulation configured set arguments: mav_id (integer, default: MAVLink identifier vehicle. Note: Multi-vehicle simulation requires extensive launch files; est (string, default: lpe, values: lpe, ekf2) estimator selection. Note overriden startup scripts craft; vehicle (string, default: clover) vehicle Depending parameter, presets loaded. Note: default clover, requires Clover-specific branch; main_camera (boolean, default: true) controls drone vision position estimation camera; rangefinder (boolean, default: true) controls drone laser rangefinder; led (boolean, default: true) controls drone programmable LED strip; gps (boolean, default: true) controls drone simulated GPS module; order start simulation, run: roslaunch clover_simulation simulator.launch start Gazebo instance gazebo_ros package), load SITL instance, spawn Clover model start Clover ROS nodes. console accessible terminal roslaunch performed. Changing simulation speed order run simulation faster slower realtime, PX4_SIM_SPEED_FACTOR environment variable, stated docs. PX4_SIM_SPEED_FACTOR set, assumed equal Note Gazebo slow simulation automatically. handled gracefully, notice Gazebo’s “Real Time Factor” significantly PX4_SIM_SPEED_FACTOR, adjust Changing initial world default, simulator.launch file start simulation resources/worlds/clover.world base world. Note real_time_update_rate set required lockstep simulation work correctly. create world simulation, derive clover.world avoid issues plugins. set world simulator.launch world_name parameter gazebo_ros instance. Configuring vehicle simulator.launch utilizes clover.launch file clover ROS package, ROS node reconfiguration real drone. reconfigured QGroundControl, real drone. parameters require rebooting drone, performed shutting simulated environment restarting write parameters logs ${ROS_HOME}/eeprom/parameters ${ROS_HOME}/log, Note log directory naming schema logs ROS: creates log directories based current date, simple find. Throttling camera plugin (throttling_camera) default, Gazebo camera sensors update_rate parameter upper bound actual rate. result rates expected. fine object recognition tasks camera primary positioning sensor, desirable case, camera position calculation. provide Gazebo-ROS plugin camera sensor throttle simulation maintain update rate. plugin API based gazebo_ros_camera plugin, respects parameters SDF: <minUpdateRate> (double, default: <updateRate>) allowed publish/update rate camera <windowSize> (integer, default: number update intervals considered throttling; <maxStDev> (double, default: maximum standard deviation update intervals. simulation slowed average update rate (averaged <windowSize> samples) <minUpdateRate> consistent (standard deviation <windowSize> samples <maxStDev>).

description: The cnn_bridge package take freeze graphs and publishes them as ROS messages
readme: Overview package support parsing convolution neural networks (CNN), publishing ROS messages. package supports detection segmentation networks. Input camera topics, OpenCV camera, video. ROS Nodes cnn_publisher ROS node opens freeze graph run images. Publishes detection type cnn_bridge/Detection Detection data. Published boxes, scores, classes. addition, header image network run statistics, hmi). segmentation type cnn_bridge/Netmask Segmentation data. Published 2-dimensional array mask values. addition, header image network run statistics, hmi). Parameters source type string required True source images run network. types inputs allowed. path video file OpenCV compatible files work). device number OpenCV device. ROS Image CompressedImage topic. option works ‘usb_cam’ (subscribes ‘usb_cam/image_raw’), ‘cv_cam’ (subscribes ‘cv_camera/image_raw’), ‘ueye’ subscribes ‘ueye_0/image_raw’). logdir type string required True Path hypes file. bellow JSON file. metadata_source type string required True Path metadata file. bellow JSON file. mode type string required True ‘detection’ ‘segmentation’ depending mode. input_tensor type string required True explanatory. output_tensor type string/[string] required True segmentation, explanatory. detection array tensors [boxes,scores,classes] display type Boolean default True display output video_save type Boolean/String default True implemented! save output False disable. True saves ‘/Camera__datetime'. string video title. node saves raw video video mask boxes overlay. running video file, raw saved. cpu type string default False implemented! Sets run network CPU Nvidia GPU present. gpu_percent type Float default Sets percentage Nvidia GPU generally running simultaneous networks. Launch File Examples Start cnn_bridge segmentation mode: roslaunch cnn_bridge segmentation_publisher.launch Start cnn_bridge detection mode: roslaunch cnn_bridge detection_publisher.launch Hypes “frozen_graph_path”: "image_height": "image_width": "resize_image": true, //TODO Add additional fields Metadata JSON segmentation mode: “classes”: [“CLASS_NAME_1”, “CLASS_NAME_2”, “CLASS_NAME_N”] detection mode: “classes”: “color”: (red, green, blue), class outputted network assigned class “id_category”: parent class class dog parent Animal) “category”: parent class class dog parent Animal)

The cob_base_controller_utils package contains common utils for various base_controllers.
The 'cob_base_velocity_smoother' reads velocity messages and publishes messages of the same type for "smoothed" velocity to avoid shaking behavior.

This package provides nodes that broadcast tf-frames along various (model-based) Cartesian paths (e.g. Linear, Circular).
    The tf-frames are interpolated using a given velocity profile (e.g. Ramp, Sinoid) and can be used as targets for the cob_frame_tracker/cob_twist_controller.
The cob_collision_velocity_filter package is a package for collision avoidance using teleoperation.
cob_control meta-package
The cob_control_mode_adapter package provides a node that automatically loads respective ros_controllers depending on required control mode.
Common messages and services used in various packages within cob_control.
The cob_footprint_observer package adjusts the footprint of the robot based on the setup (e.g. arm and/or tray).
The cob_frame_tracker package contains nodes that publish Twist commands based on the distance to the desired tf frame target.
The cob_hardware_emulation package provides idealized nodes emulating real robot hardware and/or physics simulation.

The cob_mecanum_controller_node provides a lightweight base controller for mecanum drive robots. 
    The out/input for the wheel command/state are wheel velocities in rad/s for the wheels 
    [front left, front right, rear left, rear right]
The cob_model_identifier package provides nodes to analyse the system response behavior of actuators to optimally tune PID controllers to be used with cob_twist_controller framework.

description: The cob_obstacle_distance package calculates distances between both robot links and obstacles to be used for obstacle avoidance within cob_twist_controller framework.
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot omni-directional base platform.
This package provides a trajectory controller which controlls velocities for a chain of joints. This controller can be used e.g. with [[schunk_powercube_chain]].
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot tricycle base platform.

The main purpose of the cob_twist_controller is to convert target twists into joint velocities. 
  Therefore it makes use of several implemented inverse kinematics approaches at the first order differential level. 
  The inverse differential kinematics solver considers kinematic chain extensions, singularity robustness, 
  redundancy resolution and priority-based methods.
  To avoid hardware destruction there is a limiter interface active as well. 
  Via parameter server users can dynamically configure the solving strategy.
authors: Felix Messmer, Marco Bezzon, Christoph Mark, Francisco Moreno

description: This package contains classes that are able to control the platform of the Care-O-Bot. This means to establish a CAN communication to drive and steering motors of the platform and later send motion commands and receive motor information.
Driver package for interfacing the battery management system (BMS) on Care-O-bot.

The package cob_canopen_motor implements a controller-drive component which is connected to a can-bus and works with a canopen-interface. "CanDriveItf" provides a - more or less - generic interface to the controller-drive components. "CanDrvie..." then implements a specific setup, e.g. an ELMO Harmonica Controller in case of the "CanDriveHarmonica".
The cob_driver stack includes packages that provide access to the Care-O-bot hardware through ROS messages, services and actions. E.g. for mobile base, arm, camera sensors, laser scanners, etc...
This packagae implements the special homing procedure that is needed for old cob4/raw bases

The package cob_generic_can provides an interface for nodes on a can-bus and examplary wrappers for two PeakSys-can-libs. When a can-bus-device is generated (for an example see base_dirve_chain) you can use generic_can to create as many itfs as there will be components communicating via this can-bus. Assign type of the can communication device (e.g. usb-to-can or can-card of a specific vendor) and can-address of the target device. This package comes with wrappers for PeakSys and PeakSysUSB adapters.

description: This package implements a sound play module using text2wave and aplay through python.

cob_undercarriage_ctrl implements a controller for the omnidirectional base of Care-O-bot 3 on joint level. For a given Pltf-Twist the according wheel steering angles and linear wheel velocities are calculated based on the principle of rigid body motion. Each joint is than controlled individually to achieve the computed position and velocity

Deprecated
    "cob_utilities" subsumes a number of classes, which are used in the original COb3 software. E.g. "IniFile.h" supports the original inifile structure of Care-O-bot 3. "MathSup.h" provides some basic functions like conversion from degree to radion or norming of angles within +/- PI. The package is currently used while the drivers are ported to ROS and Orocos respectively. Midterm it shall be removed and the ROS structures shall be used for reading parameters during initialization. So, don't use this package in new code!

description: Messages used by Fawkes to interact with ROS.
readme: Fawkes ROS Message Types repository ROS message, service, action specifications access Fawkes features ROS NavGraph navgraph topological graph path planning storing location-specific semantic information related ground map. message types relevant. NavGraph message: navgraph specification- NavGraphEdge sub-message: Edge representation navgraph. NavGraphNode sub-message: Node representation navgraph. NavGraphProperty sub-message: Key-value pair represent properties nodes edges. NavGraphGetPairwiseCosts service Calculates costs pairs nodes graph. Note costly operation. provide full cost specification, planning problems. NavGraphPathCost sub-message: Denote costs path nodes. description:
Defines an API for access point control based on 
    dynamic_reconfigure. Other packages must
    implement the API for various access-point models: 
    for example: hostapd_access_point for hostapd-based control or
    linksys_access_point for Linksys router web interface.

SMACH, which stands for 'state machine', is a task-level
    architecture for rapidly creating complex robot behavior. At its
    core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines.  SMACH is a new library that takes
    advantage of very old concepts in order to quickly create robust
    robot behavior with maintainable and modular code.
This package containes numerous examples of how to use SMACH. See the examples directory.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys WRT610Nv2 access point with
    a dd-wrt firmware. Other access points models/dd-wrt versions
    may be compatible as long as the web interface is identical.

A ROS node that controls a hostapd-based access
    point. It is mainly intended for use with a wireless 
    network adapter running in master mode. It implements 
    the dynamic_reconfigure interface defined
    in the [[access_point_control]] package.

This package provides mapping from frequencies to
    IEEE802.11 channels and vice-versa.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys access point with
    a Linksys WRT610n-compatible web interface.
The linux_networking package
sdsdddsdsds

Test suite for the packages that are part of the "WiFi Test Setup" project:
    network_monitor_udp, network_traffic_control, hostapd_access_point, linksys_access_point,
    ddwrt_access_point.

A ROS node that watches a given network interface and publishes
     whether it is both UP and RUNNING (indicating that a cable is
     plugged into it and communication is happening, for instance) or
     not.

Facilities to monitor a network connection by sending UDP packets from
    a client to a server, which bounces them back to the client. The client
    collects statistics on latency and loss. The server is a C standalone utility
    or a ROS node. The client can be a ROS node, a standalone utility or a python class.
A ROS node that allows control of network emulation parameters such as bandwidth, loss and latency for a Linux network interface.

Merging multiple 3D maps, represented as pointclouds,
  without knowledge of initial positions of robots.

The mbf_recovery_behaviors package

The Move Base Flex (MBF) recovery behavior moves the robot back for specified length.
        It also checks the costmap for a possible collision behind the robot while moving and
        stops the robot if necessary.
readme: Moveback recovery simple recovery behavior moves robot implements move base flex recovery interface. readme: ROS上のOMRON SENTECHカメラSDK omronsentech_cameraパッケージはオムロン 本パッケージ対応カメラはLinux版のSentechSDK対応カメラと同様です。 カメラの機能やGenICam GenApiノードのアクセスはSentechSDKのカメラ制御機能(Transport Layer) GenICam GenApiノードとROSにあるノードとは全く別の物であることを注意下さい。 各GenICam GenApiノードは、汎用インタフェースを介してアクセス可能なカメラデバイスの機能またはレジスタに対応しています。GenICam GenApiの詳細は[GenICam GenApiドキュメント（英語）]を参照下さい。 本パッケージはUbuntu 64ビット上のROS-KineticとUbuntu 64ビット上のROS-Melodicで動作確認済みです。 SentechSDKのカメラ制御機能(Transport Layer) モジュールを利用するため、本パッケージをインストールする際は事前にSentechSDKをインストールする必要があります。下記のURLでSentechSDK (USB3VisionとGigEVision)がダウンロードできます： https://sentech.co.jp/products/USB/software.html. 本パッケージは下記のROSパッケージが必要となります： camera_info_manager image_transport message_generation sensor_msgs std_msgs pluginlib nodelet roscpp roslaunch roslint rospy インストール手順として、catkinのワークスペースにomronsentech_camera.gitをクローンするかこのレポジトリに全ファイルをコピーして下さい。 通常のROSパッケージとしてパッケージをビルドする前にSentechSDKの環境変数の設定を確認して下さい: echo $STAPI_ROOT_PATH 環境変数が未設定の場合は、下記のコマンドを実行して下さい（SentechSDKを/opt/sentechにインストールした場合）： source /opt/sentech/.stprofile omronsentech_cameraの内部構成は以下のとおりです。 |-./README.md |-./README-ja.md |-./CMakeLists.txt |-./nodelet_plugins.xml |-./package.xml |-./rosdoc.yaml |-./doc/ |-./html/ |-./mainpage-en.md |-./mainpage-ja.md |-./manifest.yaml |-./examples/ |-./cpp/grabber.cpp |-./python/grabber.py |-./include/ |-./omronsentech_camera/*.h |-./launch/ |-./*.launch |-./*.yaml |-./msg/ |-./*.msg |-./src/ |-./omronsentech_camera/*.cpp |-./srv/ |-./*.srv docフォルダにあるドキュメントを生成する場合、omronsentech_cameraフォルダで下記のコマンドを実行下さい： rosdoc_lite doc 注意：Doxygenとrosdoc_liteパッケージが必要となります。 stcamera_nodeノード パッケージにあるstcamera_nodeはパラメータcamera_to_connectにより自動的に最初見つけたカメラ、指定したカメラや全カメラと接続し、画像を取得します。 stcamera_nodeノードの実行は次のコマンドのいずれかを実行下さい： rosrun omronsentech_camera stcamera_node roslaunch omronsentech_camera stcamera_node.launch ノードをstcamera_node.launchファイルスクリプトで起動するとカメラ接続パラメータがstcamera_node.yamlで簡単に設定できます。 camera_to_connect 接続可能なカメラの設定。このパラメーターは、stcamera_nodeが実行される前に設定する必要があります。 実行中にこのパラメーターを変更しても、その変更は反映されません。設定無しの場合、最初に見つけたカメラを接続します。”all”を設定する場合、見つけた全てのカメラを接続します。CAMERA_ID又はCAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)を設定する場合、指定したカメラのみと接続します。例： camera_to_connect:[]: camera_to_connect:["all"]: camera_to_connect:["00:11:1c:f6:yy:xx","STC-MCS510U3V(00XXYY0)"]: MACアドレス「00:11:1c:f6:yy:xx」のGigEVisionカメラとシリアル番号「00XXYY0」のUSB3Visionカメラ(STC-MCS510U3V)のみ接続します。 camera_to_connect:["14210003XXYY"]: ID「14210003XXYY」のカメラのみ接続します. default_calibration_file 指定するキャリブレーションファイルがないカメラを利用するデフォールトキャリブレーションファイルパス。stcamera_nodeが実行中にこのパラメータが変更され、カメラもすでに接続されている場合は、変更を適用するためにカメラの画像取得を無効にして再度有効にして下さい。キャリブレーションファイルの作成と使い方は下記のROSのチュートリアルを参照下さい（英語）： http://wiki.ros.org/camera_calibration/Tutorials/MonocularCalibration File truncated lines full file

readme: rosthrottle ROS Python package throttling ROS nodes programatically Python. Sits top ros_comm topic_tools throttle utility. rosthrottle package types throttlers: MessageThrottle Bandwidth Throttle. MessageThrottle throttle topic message frequency basis. rosthrottle import MessageThrottle intopic 'in' outtopic 'out_throttled' rate MessageThrottle(intopic, outtopic, rate) start throttler process pid bt.start() update throttler configuration bt.update(rate=5.0) kill throttler bt.stop() BandwidthThrottle throttle topic bandwidth basis. rosthrottle import BandwidthThrottle intopic 'in' outtopic 'out_throttled' bandwidth window BandwidthThrottle(intopic, outtopic, bandwidth, window) start throttler process pid bt.start() update throttler configuration bt.update(bandwidth=100) kill throttler bt.stop() src/rosthrottle/tests/ additional examples documentation.

readme: seed_smartactuator_sdk ROS上のパッケージから本ライブラリをインクルードし、SEED-Driverへコマンドを送信するための一連サンプルを示す。 なお、PCからSEED-Driverへ直接指令を行う際は通信モジュール(CM4U/CMSU)が必要となる。 多軸ロボットへ指令する際は、aero3_command.hを用いる。詳細は seed_r7_ros_controllerを参考にされたい #include <ros/ros.h> #include "seed_smartactuator_sdk/seed3_command.h" int main(int argc, char **argv) ros::init(argc,argv,"cpp_sample_node"); ros::NodeHandle seed::controller::SeedCommand seed; seed.openPort("/dev/ttyACM0",115200); seed.openCom(); int std::array<int,3> motor_state; seed.runScript(id,1); seed.waitForScriptEnd(1); (ros::ok()) motor_state seed.getPosition(id); if(motor_state[0]) std::cout motor_state[2] std::endl; return Python #!/usr/bin/env python import rospy seed_smartactuator_sdk.seed3_command import SeedCommand main "__main__": rospy.init_node('python_sample_node') seed=SeedCommand() seed.open_port("/dev/ttyACM0",115200) seed.open_com() motor_state seed.run_script(id,1) seed.wait_for_script_end(1) rospy.is_shutdown(): motor_state seed.get_position(id) if(motor_state[0]): print motor_state[2]

description: The sr_gazebo_sim package

This is a ROS interface to the Shadow Robot's robotic hand. It
        contains both an interface to the real hand (communicating via
        a CAN interface) and a simulated version of the hand. It also
        contains an interface to Shadow Robot's muscle arm.
This package contains the actuator used in the hand model.

The sr_mechanism_controllers package contains different types of
        controllers for the etherCAT hand: position controllers,
        velocity controllers, force controllers, ...

sr_mechanism_model contains the transmissions used in the robot model. We needed specific
        transmission as we're using our own actuator. We also needed to take care of the joint 0s
        which combine the distal and middle phalanges.

An interface to the tactile sensors used in the Shadow Dextrous Hand. Also Contains a virtual set of
        sensors.

description: The swarm_ros_bridge package
readme: swarm_ros_bridge Introduction lightweight middle interface ROS package based ZeroMQ. enables ROS messages transmission swarm robots socket communication. purpose package replace traditional running ROS multiple machines ROS1, drawbacks swarm robots situation. ROS robots communicating swarm_ros_bridge shown Compared ROS1 multi-robot wireless communication, benefits: Robust: base station ROS master launching Support robot launching random sequence connecting autonomously. Flexible: choose sending/receiving ROS topics transferring topic (names) ROS1 Easy ROS topics configuration file. Compared ROS2 DDS communication, benefits: Lightweight: small ROS bridge node subscribing sending remote ROS topics, connecting ROS nodes easy. Reliable: ZeroMQ socket communication based TCP protocol ROS2 based DDS, default protocol UDP (unreliable). DDS designed data exchange native processes wired communication remote wireless communication. ROS wiki page: https://wiki.ros.org/swarm_ros_bridge source code: https://github.com/shupx/swarm_ros_bridge.git csdn blog chinese): https://blog.csdn.net/benchuspx/article/details/128576723 Structure swarm_ros_bridge CMakeLists.txt config ros_topics.yaml Config file send/receive ROS topics include bridge_node.hpp Header file bridge_node.cpp ros_sub_pub.hpp Header file ROS message type. launch test.launch package.xml src bridge_node.cpp Reliable TCP bridge ros data transfer unstable network. send/receive ROS topics ../config/ros_topics.yaml zmq socket(PUB/SUB mode), reconnects autonomously supports pub-sub connection TCP protocol. Install Supported platforms/releases: Platform ROS Release Ubuntu Xenial ROS Kinetic Ubuntu Bionic ROS Melodic Ubuntu Focal ROS Noetic Install process: clone package mkdir swarm_ros_bridge_ws/src ros workspace swarm_ros_bridge_ws/src git clone https://gitee.com/shu-peixuan/swarm_ros_bridge.git 'git clone https://github.com/shupx/swarm_ros_bridge.git' install dependencies sudo apt install libzmqpp-dev 'rosdep install --from-path swarm_ros_bridge/' build catkin_make source devel/setup.bash Usage ROS topic information config/ros_topics.yaml. sending topic, port binds “tcp://*:port”. receiving topic, port remote source port connects “tcp://srcIP:srcPort”. max_freq limit sending frequency exceeds max_freq. Set max_freq large decrease sending frequency. Launch bridge_node: roslaunch swarm_ros_bridge test.launch local machine test Publish messages send_topics check remote recv_topics receiving messages. console print INFO time recv_topics receive messages. provide simple latency test demo machines. refer scripts/README.md File truncated lines full file

readme: timed_roslaunch script delay launch roslaunch file. Usage script delay launch roslaunch file. file executable (chmod timed_roslaunch.sh) Run command line rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh [number seconds delay] [rospkg] [roslaunch file] [arguments (optional)] roslaunch timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.launch time:=[number delay] pkg:=[rospkg] file:=[roslaunch file] value:=[arguments (optional)] rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" Run roslaunch file <launch> <include file="$(find timed_roslaunch)/launch/timed_roslaunch.launch"> <arg name="time" <arg name="pkg" value="turtlebot_navigation" <arg name="file" value="amcl_demo.launch" <arg name="value" value="initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" <arg name="node_name" value="timed_roslaunch" optional jBrgment </include> </launch> <launch> <node pkg="timed_roslaunch" type="timed_roslaunch.sh" args="2 turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0"  name="timed_roslaunch" output="screen" </launch> LICENSE software released BSD 3-Clause License, LICENSE.txt.

description:
A light-weight, Eigen-based
    C++ library for trajectory optimization for legged robots. 
    This library provides implementations for variables, costs and constraints
    that can be used to represent a legged locomotion problem. 
    The resulting Nonlinear Programming Problem (NLP) can then be solved with
    off-the-shelf solvers, e.g. Ipopt using the generic optimizer interface

description: This is a wrapper around the marker detection library ArUco.

The tuw_checkerboard package is designed to detect one 
    checkerboard and to estimate the pose of the checkerboard relative to the camera.
    The detection itself is based on the opencv functions for checkerboards.

The tuw_ellipses package contains a computer vision library which is able to detect ellipses within images.  
    The package is able to estimate the pose of the circle related to the ellipse the circle diameter as well as the camera parameter are known.
    A dynamic reconfigure interface allows the user to tune the parameter of the system to ones needs.
    But be aware that the pose of a projected circle within a image (ellipse) has two solutions and only one is published as TF.

This node does pose estimation for detected fiducials (marker_msgs/FiducialDetection.msg)

This contains CvBridge, which converts between ROS
    Image messages and OpenCV images.
    ==
    This package intentionally link against boost-python3 and
    install under /opt/ros/ROS_DISTRO/lib/python3/dist-package
    Please add
    import sys, os; sys.path.insert(0,'/opt/ros/' + os.environ['ROS_DISTRO'] + '/lib/python3/dist-packages/')
    before you call import cv_bridge

`image_geometry` contains C++ and Python libraries for interpreting images
    geometrically. It interfaces the calibration parameters in sensor_msgs/CameraInfo
    messages with OpenCV functions such as image rectification, much as cv_bridge
    interfaces ROS sensor_msgs/Image with OpenCV data types.
Tests the enumerants of the ROS Image message, and functionally tests the Python and C++ implementations of CvBridge.

description:
A multiplexer for command velocity inputs. Arbitrates incoming cmd_vel messages from several topics,
     allowing one topic at a time to command the robot, based on priorities. It also deallocates current
     allowed topic if no messages are received after a configured timeout. All topics, together with their
     priority and timeout are configured through a YAML file, that can be reload at runtime.
Library for various controller types and algorithms

A controller for driving a differential drive base to a pose goal or along a path specified by multiple poses.
    A pose consists of a 2D position (x,y) and a 1D orientation (theta).

description:
Math toolkit for Yujin open control system. This package is intended to contain common use functions,
    mostly for simple mathematics but also for tf-related conversions.
    By no means it pretends to be an efficient and robust, widely used math library, but a play ground where
    to put common code that is typically repeated in many robot control programs.

A controller ensuring the safe operation of your robot.

    The SafetyController listens to ranger readings in order to stop (and move back), if obstacles get to close.

    This controller can be enabled/disabled.

Virtual sensor that uses semantic map information to "see" obstacles undetectable by robot sensors.

      use auxiliary scripts to read and publish files' content. Data directory contains some example files.
